nouns.food.drinks.milk: Milk is a drink that helps you grow big and strong.
It is white and cold, and you pour it into a cup.
You can drink milk with breakfast or with a cookie.
nouns.food.drinks.water: Water is a clear drink your body needs every day.
It has no color and no taste, and it pours from the tap.
You drink water when you are thirsty, especially after you play.
nouns.food.drinks.juice: Juice is a sweet drink made from fruit.
It can be orange, red, or purple, and it tastes fruity.
You might have juice with breakfast or a snack.
nouns.food.drinks.smoothie: A smoothie is a thick, cold drink you sip through a straw.
It is blended from fruit and milk or yogurt until smooth.
You can have a smoothie as a yummy snack or breakfast.
nouns.food.fruit.banana: You peel the yellow part and eat the soft inside.
It is long and curved, and turns from green to yellow.
A banana is a sweet fruit that's great for a snack.
nouns.food.fruit.apple: An apple is a crunchy fruit you bite right into.
It is round and can be red, green, or yellow.
You might eat an apple as a snack or in your lunch.
nouns.food.fruit.grapes: Grapes are tiny round fruits you pop in your mouth.
They grow in bunches and can be green or purple.
Grapes make a sweet, juicy snack.
nouns.food.fruit.orange: An orange is a round fruit you peel before you eat.
It is orange-colored and splits into juicy pieces.
Oranges are sweet and full of juice.
nouns.food.fruit.strawberry: A strawberry is a sweet red fruit with tiny seeds.
It is heart-shaped with little green leaves on top.
You can eat strawberries plain or on top of other foods.
nouns.food.fruit.blueberry: Blueberries are tiny round blue fruits.
They are small enough to eat a whole handful at once.
Blueberries are sweet in cereal or pancakes.
nouns.food.veg.carrot: A carrot is a crunchy orange vegetable you can munch.
It is long and pointy and grows down in the ground.
You might dip a carrot or have it with lunch.
nouns.food.veg.broccoli: Broccoli is a green vegetable that looks like a little tree.
It has a bumpy top and a thick stem.
You eat broccoli to help your body stay healthy.
nouns.food.veg.corn: Corn is a vegetable with sweet yellow kernels.
The kernels grow in rows on a long cob.
You can eat corn on the cob or off a spoon.
nouns.food.veg.peas: Peas are tiny round green vegetables.
They grow in a long green pod, all in a row.
Peas are a soft little side at dinner.
nouns.food.snacks.cracker: A cracker is a crunchy snack you bite and crunch.
It is flat and crispy, often a little square.
You might have crackers with cheese in your snack cup.
nouns.food.snacks.cereal: Cereal is a crunchy snack you eat from a bowl.
It comes in little pieces, often with milk poured on top.
You usually eat cereal for breakfast.
nouns.food.snacks.cheese: Cheese is a soft, tasty snack made from milk.
It can be yellow or white, in slices or little cubes.
You might have cheese with crackers or in a sandwich.
nouns.food.snacks.yogurt: Yogurt is a smooth, creamy snack you eat with a spoon.
It is soft and cool and can taste fruity.
Yogurt is a good snack or breakfast.
nouns.food.snacks.cookie: A cookie is a sweet treat you bite and chew.
It is round and can have chocolate chips inside.
You might have a cookie as a special snack.
nouns.food.snacks.chips: Chips are thin, crispy, salty snacks.
They crunch loudly when you bite them.
You might have chips with lunch or at a party.
nouns.food.snacks.pretzel: A pretzel is a crunchy, salty snack.
It is often twisted into a fun knotty shape.
Pretzels make a good snack on the go.
nouns.food.meals.bread: Bread is a soft food you can eat in slices.
It is fluffy inside with a firmer crust outside.
You use bread to make toast or a sandwich.
nouns.food.meals.pasta: Pasta is a soft food you twirl on your fork.
It comes in fun shapes like noodles, shells, and spirals.
You eat pasta at dinner, often with sauce.
nouns.food.meals.pizza: Pizza is a yummy food you eat in slices.
It is round, with cheese and sauce on top of a crust.
Pizza is fun to share for dinner or a party.
nouns.food.meals.egg: An egg is a food you can eat cooked for breakfast.
It has a yellow middle and a white part around it.
You might have a scrambled egg in the morning.
nouns.food.meals.chicken: Chicken is a food you eat for dinner.
It is cooked warm and can be in strips or pieces.
You might have chicken with rice or vegetables.
nouns.food.meals.rice: Rice is a soft food made of tiny white grains.
Each grain is small, and you scoop it with a spoon.
You eat rice at dinner, often with other foods.
nouns.food.meals.sandwich: A sandwich is food you hold and bite.
It is two pieces of bread with yummy things inside.
You might have a sandwich for lunch.
nouns.food.fruit.more.watermelon: Watermelon is a big, juicy fruit you eat in slices.
It is green outside and pink inside with little black seeds.
Watermelon is sweet and cool on a hot day.
nouns.food.fruit.more.peach: A peach is a soft, fuzzy fruit you bite into.
It is round with fuzzy skin and a pit in the middle.
Peaches are sweet and juicy in the summer.
nouns.food.fruit.more.pineapple: Pineapple is a sweet, juicy fruit.
It is spiky and brown outside with a crown of leaves on top.
You eat pineapple in juicy yellow chunks.
nouns.food.fruit.more.mango: A mango is a soft, sweet, juicy fruit.
It has smooth skin and a big flat pit inside.
Mango tastes sweet and a little tangy.
nouns.food.fruit.more.kiwi: A kiwi is a small fruit with a fuzzy brown skin.
Inside it is bright green with tiny black seeds.
You scoop out the soft green part with a spoon.
nouns.food.fruit.more.cherry: A cherry is a tiny round red fruit.
It has a little stem and a small pit inside.
Cherries are sweet, and you eat them one by one.
nouns.food.fruit.more.pear: A pear is a sweet fruit shaped like a teardrop.
It is green or yellow with soft, juicy insides.
You might have a pear as a snack.
nouns.food.veg.more.tomato: A tomato is a round, juicy red food.
It is smooth and shiny, soft when you bite it.
You might have tomato in a salad or on a sandwich.
nouns.food.veg.more.potato: A potato is a vegetable that grows in the ground.
It is round or oval with a brown skin.
Potatoes can be mashed, baked, or made into fries.
nouns.food.veg.more.cucumber: A cucumber is a long, green, crunchy vegetable.
It is cool and watery inside.
You might have cucumber slices with a dip.
nouns.food.veg.more.lettuce: Lettuce is a crunchy green vegetable with big leaves.
The leaves are light green and a little crinkly.
You put lettuce in a salad or a sandwich.
nouns.food.veg.more.onion: An onion is a round vegetable with papery skin.
It has layers inside and a strong smell.
Onion is cooked into food to give it flavor.
nouns.food.veg.more.pepper: A pepper is a crunchy vegetable that can be red, green, or yellow.
It is shiny outside and hollow inside.
You might have pepper slices with a dip or in dinner.
nouns.food.veg.more.mushroom: A mushroom is a soft vegetable shaped like a little umbrella.
It has a round top and a short stem.
Mushrooms are cooked into pizza, pasta, or soup.
nouns.food.veg.more.celery: Celery is a long, crunchy green vegetable.
It is a thin stalk with a stringy crunch.
You might have celery with peanut butter as a snack.
nouns.food.treats.ice_cream: Ice cream is a cold, sweet treat you lick or spoon.
It is creamy and comes in many flavors and colors.
You might have ice cream on a hot day as a treat.
nouns.food.treats.cake: Cake is a soft, sweet treat for special days.
It is fluffy with frosting on top.
You eat cake at birthdays and parties.
nouns.food.treats.candy: Candy is a small, very sweet treat.
It comes in bright colors and fun shapes.
You might have a piece of candy as a special treat.
nouns.food.treats.popcorn: Popcorn is a light, crunchy snack.
Little kernels pop into fluffy white puffs.
You might have popcorn at a movie.
nouns.food.treats.donut: A donut is a sweet, soft treat with a hole in the middle.
It is round with frosting or sprinkles on top.
You might have a donut as a special breakfast treat.
nouns.food.treats.lollipop: A lollipop is a hard candy on a stick.
It is round and colorful, and you lick it.
A lollipop is a fun, slow treat.
nouns.food.treats.chocolate: Chocolate is a sweet brown treat that melts in your mouth.
It is smooth and comes in bars or little pieces.
You might have chocolate as a special treat.
nouns.food.condiments.ketchup: Ketchup is a red sauce you put on food.
It is smooth and a little sweet and tangy.
You might dip fries or a hot dog in ketchup.
nouns.food.condiments.butter: Butter is a soft, yellow spread.
It is creamy and melts when it gets warm.
You spread butter on bread or toast.
nouns.food.condiments.syrup: Syrup is a sweet, sticky liquid you pour on food.
It is brown and slow and gooey.
You pour syrup on pancakes or waffles.
nouns.food.condiments.jam: Jam is a sweet, fruity spread.
It is thick and made from fruit, often red or purple.
You spread jam on toast or in a sandwich.
nouns.food.condiments.salt: Salt is a tiny white seasoning that adds flavor.
It comes in tiny grains you sprinkle on food.
A little salt makes food taste yummier.
nouns.food.condiments.pepper: Pepper is a spice that adds a little kick to food.
It comes in tiny dark specks you sprinkle on.
A pinch of pepper makes dinner taste better.
nouns.food.breakfast.pancake: A pancake is a soft, round breakfast food.
It is flat and fluffy and stacks up on a plate.
You pour syrup on pancakes in the morning.
nouns.food.breakfast.waffle: A waffle is a breakfast food with little squares.
The squares hold pockets of syrup.
You might have a waffle in the morning.
nouns.food.breakfast.muffin: A muffin is a soft, sweet breakfast treat.
It is round with a fluffy top, sometimes with berries.
You might have a muffin for breakfast or a snack.
nouns.food.breakfast.bagel: A bagel is a chewy, round bread with a hole.
It is firm outside and soft inside.
You might have a bagel with cream cheese for breakfast.
nouns.food.breakfast.oatmeal: Oatmeal is a warm, soft breakfast you eat with a spoon.
It is thick and creamy and can have fruit on top.
You might have oatmeal on a cold morning.
nouns.food.meals.more.hamburger: A hamburger is food you hold with both hands.
It is a round patty inside a soft bun.
You might have a hamburger for lunch or dinner.
nouns.food.meals.more.hot_dog: A hot dog is a food you hold and bite.
It is a long sausage inside a soft bun.
You might have a hot dog at a cookout.
nouns.food.meals.more.taco: A taco is a food you hold and bite.
It is a folded shell filled with yummy things.
You might have tacos for dinner.
nouns.food.meals.more.burrito: A burrito is food wrapped up in a soft tortilla.
It is rolled up with the filling tucked inside.
You hold a burrito in your hands to eat it.
nouns.food.meals.more.soup: Soup is a warm food you eat with a spoon.
It is a warm liquid with bits of food floating in it.
You might have soup when it's cold or you feel sick.
nouns.food.meals.more.salad: A salad is a fresh, crunchy mix of vegetables.
It has leafy greens and colorful veggies in a bowl.
You might have a salad with lunch or dinner.
nouns.food.meals.more.noodles: Noodles are long, soft strands you slurp.
They are squiggly and you twirl them on a fork.
You might have noodles in soup or with sauce.
nouns.food.meals.more.fries: Fries are crispy, salty potato sticks.
They are long and golden and fun to dip.
You might have fries with your lunch.
nouns.food.meals.more.dumpling: A dumpling is a soft little pocket of food.
It is doughy outside with yummy filling inside.
You might have dumplings for dinner.
nouns.food.meals.more.sushi: Sushi is a small, fun food you eat in one bite.
It is rolled up with rice and other things inside.
You might have sushi as a special dinner.
nouns.food.fruit.extra.lemon: A lemon is a sour yellow fruit.
It is oval and bumpy with very sour juice.
People squeeze lemon into drinks and food.
nouns.food.fruit.extra.lime: A lime is a sour green fruit.
It is small and round with tangy juice.
People squeeze lime into drinks and food.
nouns.food.fruit.extra.avocado: An avocado is a soft, green fruit.
It has bumpy skin and a big round pit inside.
You scoop out the creamy green part to eat.
nouns.food.fruit.extra.coconut: A coconut is a big, hard, brown fruit.
It is round and hairy with sweet white inside.
Coconut can be in treats and drinks.
nouns.food.fruit.extra.plum: A plum is a small, juicy fruit.
It is round and smooth with purple or red skin.
Plums are sweet and a little tangy.
nouns.food.fruit.extra.pomegranate: A pomegranate is a round red fruit full of seeds.
Inside are lots of tiny, juicy red seeds.
You eat the little seeds a handful at a time.
nouns.food.veg.extra.spinach: Spinach is a leafy green vegetable.
It has soft, dark green leaves.
Spinach is healthy in a salad or cooked in food.
nouns.food.veg.extra.eggplant: An eggplant is a smooth, purple vegetable.
It is shiny and shaped like a big teardrop.
nouns.food.veg.extra.zucchini: Zucchini is a long, green vegetable.
It is smooth and shaped like a thick stick.
nouns.food.veg.extra.cauliflower: Cauliflower is a white vegetable that looks like a little tree.
It has a bumpy top, like white broccoli.
nouns.food.veg.extra.squash: Squash is a vegetable that can be yellow or green.
It is smooth and comes in fun shapes.
Squash is cooked soft for dinner.
nouns.food.veg.extra.asparagus: Asparagus is a long, thin green vegetable.
It is a skinny stalk with a pointy tip.
Asparagus is cooked as a side at dinner.
